description_long: This is a standard regulatory speed limit sign displaying the number 60 within a red circle on a white rectangular background, indicating a maximum speed of 60 kilometers per hour. The sign face is mounted on a tall, slender, dark-colored metal post, which is cylindrical and uniform in diameter from top to bottom. The sign plate is flat and appears to be made of reflective metal or coated aluminum, designed for high visibility in various lighting conditions. The post is likely galvanized steel, providing durability and resistance to weathering. The rear of the sign is plain and unmarked, showing only the back of the metal plate and the supporting post. This type of sign is commonly found along urban and suburban roadsides to regulate vehicle speed and enhance road safety.
description: A rectangular speed limit sign with '60' in black inside a red circle, mounted on a tall, dark metal pole. The sign is designed for roadside placement and high visibility.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the plain back of the sign plate and the full length of the dark cylindrical post.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the sign plate and the straight, narrow post.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the '60' speed limit in black within a red circle on a white background, mounted on a dark pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slim profile of the sign and post.
